OSChina.title: MiniMax M2.7 开源协议引争议：严禁商业用途，开发者质疑“伪开源”
OSChina.description: 国产AI大模型厂商MiniMax（稀宇科技）于近日开源了其最新一代模型MiniMax-M2.7，该模型在编程能力基准测试SWE-Pro中取得56.22%的成绩，几乎追平Anthropic的Claude Opus水平。 然而，这本应值得庆贺的技术突破却因开源协议的突然"变脸"在开发者社区引发轩然大波——MiniMax M2.7明确禁止商业用途，除非获得公司书面授权，...

OSChina.title: Git for Windows 2.53.0(3) 发布
OSChina.description: Git for Windows v2.53.0(3) 现已发布。这是一个安全修复版本，解决了 CVE-2026-32631 漏洞： CVE-2026-32631，Git for Windows：当用户克隆包含指向网络驱动器的符号链接的仓库时，Git 会在检出过程中跟随这些符号链接，导致 Windows 系统透明地执行 NTLM 身份验证，并将用户的 NTLMv2 哈希值泄露给攻击者控制的服务器。...

OSChina.title: 百度 Create 大会官宣三大核心看点，国内最大 AI 开发者嘉年华 5 月北京揭幕
OSChina.description: 5月13日至14日，国内最大规模的AI开发者嘉年华——Create2026百度AI开发者大会将在北京拉开帷幕。本届大会以“万物一体”为主题，首次将“Create百度AI开发者大会”与“云智大会”合二为一，集中展示百度智能云AI Infra与Agent Infra重大技术突破、“超级个体”生产力工具链、龙虾全家桶全场景智能体验三大核心看点。对于...

OSChina.title: 以 Nano-vLLM 为例，深入理解 LLM 推理引擎
OSChina.description: 这篇文章没有停留在理论层面，而是深入 Nano-vLLM ------ 一个由 DeepSeek 贡献者开源的、仅 1200 行代码却完整实现 vLLM 核心机制的推理引擎。从生产者 - 消费者调度器如何平衡吞吐量与延迟，到 BlockManager 如何通过哈希实现前缀缓存，再到张量并行下 Leader-Worker 的共享内存通信，文章用清晰的工程视角拆解了从提示...

Changelog.title: Astral has been acquired by OpenAI
Changelog.description: Astral is joining OpenAI, which says a lot about where the center of gravity is moving for developer tools, LiteLLM got hit by a nasty supply-chain attack, and OpenCode blew up as the latest serious open source swing at the coding-agent stack. We've also got Rust doing a very public reality check on

Changelog.title: From Tailnet to platform
Changelog.description: Adam talks with Tailscale co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer David Carney about where Tailscale is headed next: TSIDP, TSNet, multiple tailnets, and Aperture. They get into clickless auth (via TSIDP), TSNet apps, multiple tailnets for isolation and control, and Aperture, Tailscale’s private AI ga

Changelog.title: Big change brings big change
Changelog.description: This week's been wild — Iran bombed AWS data centers to take down Claude, OpenAI dropped GPT-5.4 (and it's seriously good for coding), and living brain cells are literally playing DOOM. We've also got a heartfelt take on what it feels like to be a 10x engineer in the age of AI, plus some cool new to

Changelog.title: Finale & Friends
Changelog.description: Adam and Jerod get into the news, Jerod officially retires from the pod (and Changelog), plus a bonus for our Changelog++ subs!

Changelog.title: Opus 4.5 changed everything
Changelog.description: Burke Holland works on GitHub Copilot by day and codes with his AI agents always. Early January, Burke posted about how Opus 4.5 changed everything. We were all still buzzing from the holiday-season 2x usage bump Claude gave us, and Opus 4.5 felt like a genuine step function in capability. Burke and

Changelog.title: The mythical agent-month
Changelog.description: Wes McKinney on the mythical agent-month, install Peon Ping to employ a Peon today, Andreas Kling explains why Ladybird is adopting Rust, Cloudflare has a new MCP server that's quite efficient, and Elliot Bonneville thinks the only moat left is money.

Changelog.title: Selling SDKs in the era of many Claudes
Changelog.description:
Steve Ruiz joins us for a deep-dive on tldraw (a very good free whiteboard) and the business he's built selling SDKs that help others build very good whiteboards (and more) with tldraw's high-performance web canvas.

Along the way, we discuss the excitement/fear we share about keeping our agents b

Changelog.title: All the Claw things
Changelog.description: Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI, ZeroClaw is "claw done right", MimiClaw runs on a $5 chip, Steve Yegge on managing the AI Vampire, and the day the telnet died.

Changelog.title: Han shot first
Changelog.description: Our ol' friend, Brett Cannon, is back to talk all things Python. But first! Star Wars, Machete Order, Lost, Babylon 5, Game of Thrones, Murderbot, Ted Lasso, Project Hail Mary, David Attenborough, perpetual voice rights, and the AI uncanny valley.

Changelog.title: Building the machine that builds the machine
Changelog.description:
Paul Dix joins us to discuss the InfluxDB co-founder's journey adapting to an agentic world. Paul sent his AI coding agents on various real-world side quests and shares all his findings: what's going to prod, what's not, and why he's (at least for a bit) back to coding by hand.

Update: He's back

Anthropic News.title: Anthropic’s Long-Term Benefit Trust appoints Vas Narasimhan to Board of Directors
Anthropic News.description: Vas Narasimhan has been appointed to Anthropic's Board of Directors by the Anthropic Long-Term Benefit Trust. He is a physician-scientist and the Chief Executive Officer of Novartis—one of the world's leading innovative medicines companies—and shares Anthropic’s conviction that healthcare and life s

Anthropic News.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute
Anthropic News.description: We have signed a new agreement with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity that we expect to come online starting in 2027. This significant expansion of our compute infrastructure will power our frontier Claude models and help us serve extraordinary demand from cu

Anthropic News.title: Australian government and Anthropic sign MOU for AI safety and research
Anthropic News.description: Today, Anthropic signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Australian government to cooperate on AI safety research and support the goals of Australia’s National AI Plan. Our CEO, Dario Amodei, met with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese to formalize the agreement during a visit to Canberra, Austra

Anthropic News.title: Anthropic invests $100 million into the Claude Partner Network
Anthropic News.description: We’re launching the Claude Partner Network, a program for partner organizations helping enterprises adopt Claude. We’re committing an initial $100 million to support our partners with training courses, dedicated technical support, and joint market development. Partners who join from today will get i

Anthropic News.title: Introducing The Anthropic Institute
Anthropic News.description: We’re launching The Anthropic Institute, a new effort to confront the most significant challenges that powerful AI will pose to our societies. The Anthropic Institute will draw on research from across Anthropic to provide information that other researchers and the public can use during our transitio

Anthropic News.title: Sydney will become Anthropic’s fourth office in Asia-Pacific
Anthropic News.description: Anthropic is expanding to Australia and New Zealand. In the coming weeks, we will open an office in Sydney—our fourth office in Asia-Pacific, alongside Tokyo, Bengaluru, and Seoul. The expansion reflects strong demand from businesses in Australia and New Zealand and will help us better serve the cou

Anthropic News.title: Partnering with Mozilla to improve Firefox’s security
Anthropic News.description: AI models can now independently identify high-severity vulnerabilities in complex software. As we recently documented, Claude found more than 500 zero-day vulnerabilities (security flaws that are unknown to the software’s maintainers) in well-tested open-source software.In this post, we share detail

Anthropic News.title: Where things stand with the Department of War
Anthropic News.description: A statement from Dario AmodeiYesterday (March 4) Anthropic received a letter from the Department of War confirming that we have been designated as a supply chain risk to America’s national security.As we wrote on Friday, we do not believe this action is legally sound, and we see no choice but to cha

Anthropic News.title: Statement on the comments from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth
Anthropic News.description: Earlier today, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth shared on X that he is directing the Department of War to designate Anthropic a supply chain risk. This action follows months of negotiations that reached an impasse over two exceptions we requested to the lawful use of our AI model, Claude: the mass dome

Anthropic News.title: Statement from Dario Amodei on our discussions with the Department of War
Anthropic News.description: I believe deeply in the existential importance of using AI to defend the United States and other democracies, and to defeat our autocratic adversaries.Anthropic has therefore worked proactively to deploy our models to the Department of War and the intelligence community. We were the first frontier A

HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: RLVR improves reasoning in large language models, but its effectiveness is often limited by severe reward sparsity on hard problems. HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Habitat-GS: A High-Fidelity Navigation Simulator with Dynamic Gaussian Splatting
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Training embodied AI agents depends critically on the visual fidelity of simulation environments and the ability to model dynamic humans. Current simulators rely on mesh-based rasterization with limited visual realism, and their support for dynamic human avatars, where available, is constrained to m
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Ziyuan Xia, Jingyi Xu, Chong Cui, Yuanhong Yu, Jiazhao Zhang, Qingsong Yan, Tao Ni, Junbo Chen, Xiaowei Zhou, Hujun Bao, Ruizhen Hu, Sida Peng

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Self-Adversarial One Step Generation via Condition Shifting
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: The push for efficient text to image synthesis has moved the field toward one step sampling, yet existing methods still face a three way tradeoff among fidelity, inference speed, and training efficiency. Approaches that rely on external discriminators can sharpen one step performance, but they often
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Deyuan Liu, Peng Sun, Yansen Han, Zhenglin Cheng, Chuyan Chen, Tao Lin

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Generative Refinement Networks for Visual Synthesis
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: While diffusion models dominate the field of visual generation, they are computationally inefficient, applying a uniform computational effort regardless of different complexity. In contrast, autoregressive (AR) models are inherently complexity-aware, as evidenced by their variable likelihoods, but a
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Jian Han, Jinlai Liu, Jiahuan Wang, Bingyue Peng, Zehuan Yuan

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Lightning OPD: Efficient Post-Training for Large Reasoning Models with Offline On-Policy Distillation
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: On-policy distillation (OPD) has emerged as an efficient post-training paradigm for large language models. However, standard OPD requires a live teacher inference server throughout training, resulting in substantial infrastructure overhead. In this work, we investigate whether on-policy distillation
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Yecheng Wu, Song Han, Hai Cai

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Accelerating Speculative Decoding with Block Diffusion Draft Trees
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive language models by using a lightweight drafter to propose multiple future tokens, which the target model then verifies in parallel. DFlash shows that a block diffusion drafter can generate an entire draft block in a single forward pass and achieve stat
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Liran Ringel, Yaniv Romano

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: GlotOCR Bench: OCR Models Still Struggle Beyond a Handful of Unicode Scripts
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Optical character recognition (OCR) has advanced rapidly with the rise of vision-language models, yet evaluation has remained concentrated on a small cluster of high- and mid-resource scripts. We introduce GlotOCR Bench, a comprehensive benchmark evaluating OCR generalization across 100+ Unicode scr
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Amir Hossein Kargaran, Nafiseh Nikeghbal, Jana Diesner, François Yvon, Hinrich Schütze

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Learning Versatile Humanoid Manipulation with Touch Dreaming
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Humanoid robots promise general-purpose assistance, yet real-world humanoid loco-manipulation remains challenging because it requires whole-body stability, dexterous hands, and contact-aware perception under frequent contact changes. In this work, we study dexterous, contact-rich humanoid loco-manip
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Yaru Niu, Zhenlong Fang, Binghong Chen, Shuai Zhou, Revanth Senthilkumaran, Hao Zhang, Bingqing Chen, Chen Qiu, H. Eric Tseng, Jonathan Francis, Ding Zhao

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: Masked by Consensus: Disentangling Privileged Knowledge in LLM Correctness
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Humans use introspection to evaluate their understanding through private internal states inaccessible to external observers. We investigate whether large language models possess similar privileged knowledge about answer correctness, information unavailable through external observation. We train corr
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Tomer Ashuach, Liat Ein-Dor, Shai Gretz, Yoav Katz, Yonatan Belinkov

HuggingFace Daily Papers.title: VideoFlexTok: Flexible-Length Coarse-to-Fine Video Tokenization
HuggingFace Daily Papers.description: Visual tokenizers map high-dimensional raw pixels into a compressed representation for downstream modeling. Beyond compression, tokenizers dictate what information is preserved and how it is organized. A de facto standard approach to video tokenization is to represent a video as a spatiotemporal 3D
HuggingFace Daily Papers.author: Andrei Atanov, Jesse Allardice, Roman Bachmann, Oğuzhan Fatih Kar, R Devon Hjelm, David Griffiths, Peter Fu, Afshin Dehghan, Amir Zamir

The new WQ_AFFN_CACHE_SHARD affinity scope can reduce some contention on such systems and help achieve greater p
Phoronix.author: Michael Larabel

Phoronix.title: Linux 7.1 Is A Big Win For Intel Panther Lake With FRED Now Enabled By Default

Hours after that story was published, an Intel engineer posted the patch to enable FR
Phoronix.author: Michael Larabel

Today's article features the latest Arc Pro B70 benchmarks under Linux in looking at how the
Phoronix.author: Michael Larabel

The scheduling model in turn is based in part on Apple's quality of service classes used by iOS for classifying software as user interactive, user initiative,

LWN.title: FSF clarifies its stance on AGPLv3 additional terms
LWN.description:
OnlyOffice CEO Lev Bannov has recently
claimed that the Euro-Office fork of the
OnlyOffice suite violates the GNU Affero General Public License
version 3 (AGPLv3). Krzysztof Siewicz of the Free Software
Foundation (FSF) has published
an article on the FSF's position on adding terms to the AGPLv3. In
LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: [$] Forking Vim to avoid LLM-generated code
LWN.description:
Many people dislike the proliferation of Large Language Models (LLMs) in recent
years, and so make an understandable attempt to avoid them.
That may not be possible in general, but there are two new forks of
Vim that seek to provide an editing
environment with no LLM-generated code. EVi focuses on b
LWN.author: daroc

LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: Zig 0.16.0 released
LWN.description:
The Zig project has announced version
0.16.0 of the Zig programming language.
This release features 8 months of work: changes
from 244 different contributors, spread among
1183 commits.
Perhaps most notably, this release debuts I/O
as an Interface, but don't sleep on the Language
Changes or enhancem
LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: [$] Tagging music with MusicBrainz Picard
LWN.description:
Part of the "fun" that comes with curating a self-hosted music library is tagging
music so that it has accurate and uniform metadata, such as the band names, album titles,
cover images, and so on. This can be a tedious endeavor, but there are quite a few
open-source tools to make this process easier
LWN.author: jzb

LWN.title: OpenSSL 4.0.0 released
LWN.description:
Version 4.0.0 of the OpenSSL cryptographic library has been released.  This
release includes support for a number of new cryptographic algorithms and
has a number of incompatible changes as well; see the announcement for the
details.
LWN.author: corbet

The OpenWrt One is a
router powered by the open-source firmware from the OpenWrt project; it was also the

given by Denver Gingerich of the Software Freedom Conservancy (SFC),
which played a big role in developing the router.  Gingerich returned to
the conference

LWN.title: Servo now on crates.io

The Servo project has announced
the first release of servo as a crate for use as a
library.
As you can see from the version number, this release is not a 1.0
release. In fact, we still haven't finished discussing what 1.0 means
for Servo. Nevertheless, the increased version number reflects our
growi
